0.0: Autumn closing on Upperton -- the harvest in and short, the river running high and brown, the roads quieting before the dark half of the year. Time aspect; space and identity are sibling blocks at these same addresses. A single moment held here, autumn-evening.

1.0: Up on the brow this week the lift's been half-jammed since a rope fouled the drum midweek, so the market's crowded and goods are piling, waiting their turn down.
Today a party came up off the south road and the lift before noon -- strangers with coin, so the stalls have stayed open past their hour.
1.1: This hour the upper crew work the one sound cage hard, the lift-master turning back all who can't show cause, the queue grumbling.

2.0: This past hour the inn's filled with the newcomers, loud and spending, the keeper run off her feet.

1.0: Right now a dice game is gathering at the long table, the fiddler tuning, the fire built high, the room loud.
This minute the dice are out and the stakes going down; a hooded local has drifted close, watching hands.
1.1: These seconds a thumb worries the scratched-out name in the wood, waiting a throw.

2.0: These seconds a forgotten tankard slides a finger's width toward the heaped coins as the bench is jostled.
2.1: This instant the bent southern coin flashes in the firelight as a hand sweeps the stakes -- gone.

1.2: This minute the pot comes to the boil, someone feeds the fire, sparks chase up the flue.
1.3: This minute the keeper draws a tray, bawling for the boy, chalking a tally she'll lose.

2.2: Right now bread and pottage go out hard for the newcomers, the oven roaring, the back door propped for air.

3.0: Right now it's dark and still below; a cask tapped earlier runs slow into the dark.
3.1: This minute the draught works through the bad seam, as it always does, stirring nothing.

2.4: Right now the let rooms sit cold and empty; the newcomers will want beds by midnight, none taken yet.

1.3: This hour the stepped lanes throng with folk climbing home before the light goes.

1.2: Today the shrine is quiet; the bell was rung at noon for a herdsman lost on the moor road.
1.3: Today the headman hears a quarrel over lift-priority -- who rides up first while the one cage runs.

2.0: In the cliff this week the watch is doubled since the lift fouled; the crown is content to see the towns lean on its stair.

1.0: Today the watchers track the newcomers and the press at the brink, calling notes back along the rock.
1.1: This hour a lone guard half-sleeps in the niche, the gorge wind full in his face.
1.2: This hour the loop bears on the lift, though none watch it close with the cage half-stopped.

2.0: Today the royal house keeps to the warm deep rooms, an audience set for after dark.
2.1: This hour the chamber's readied -- rushes laid, the stone chair dusted -- for a petition on the fouled lift.
2.2: This hour the ruler sits alone, the sky-shaft greying overhead, resolving nothing yet.

3.0: Today the stair stays open but watched; every passage between the towns goes past two gates and a tally.
3.1: This hour the upper grille is down, passing only the known.
3.2: This hour the lower gate is quiet -- one guard, the murder-hole above unminded.

2.4: Today the portcullis stays down; the drawbridge will not be lowered for common traffic while the watch is doubled.

3.0: On the gorge floor this week the river runs high with the autumn rain, and the lift jam has loads piling at the foot with no way up.

1.0: Today the mill runs flat out on the new grain, the wheel loud, sacks stacked for the lift that can't take them.
1.1: This hour the stones are turning and the miller's white to the elbow, the tally growing.
1.2: This hour the race is fast and full, the wheel slapping, the sluice watched against the high water.

3.2: Today the tan-pits are turned and the racks full; the stink rides the wind off downstream.

3.0: Today the foot-yard is choked with loads that can't rise; the down-crew works the one cage flat out.
3.1: This hour the bottom crew haul to the down-call, listening for the upper horn, the brake smoking.

3.4: Today the river dwellings are smoke and supper, the day's work done, the cliff's shadow already over them.

4.0: This week the lift is crippled -- the east drum fouled midweek, the west cage running double duty, the ropes complaining.

1.0: Today the sound cage climbs heavy and slow, packed each trip, watched by the crowds top and bottom.
1.1: This hour the platform's loaded past safe -- six and a netted load -- every rail gripped white.
1.2: This hour the frayed grip-line takes more hands than it should, none trusting the rail.

2.0: Today the west cage sinks as the sound one climbs, near-empty, the counterweight singing on the line.
2.1: This hour the falling platform bears two down, both silent, the rock sliding up past them.

4.3: Today a man at each drum eyes the tarred cables hard for wear since the fouling.

5.0: This week the span stays raised more than not; with the watch doubled the hold keeps itself an island.
5.1: Today the bridge is up; the oak roadway stands empty over the cut.
5.2: Today the quarried chasm lies in shadow, the river a thread far below.

6.0: This week the river's in autumn spate, brown and fast, the ford upstream barely passable.
6.1: Today the floor path is wet and flood-marked, used only by those who must.
6.2: Today the ford runs high and hard; the watch there turns back all but the known.
